title: Learning Go by doing programming tasks
exerpt: Using a hands-on apprach instead of watching courses and reading books to learn a new language.
content:
This is a challenge to complete one (or more if I'm feeling good about myself) programming task(s) a day, but the catch is that I can only do it in Go. I have never used Go before this, so I'm doing this in hopes of getting fluent at it eventually. As I complete tasks, I will note down the things I've learnt in the Github repository README together with the date of completion to track progress.

I'm learning Go because:
- It's supposedly great for web development
- I use statically typed languages a lot (and I prefer to use them over dynamically typed languages)
- Go doesn't have excessive boilerplate code unlike Ruby
- Fast compile times

The repository at https://github.com/Iscaraca/codetask-Go is constantly updated every day with new problems and my solutions in Go. With this self-directed method of learning forcing me to do research, read the docs and apply what I've learnt, I can hopefully be more fluent so I can continue with using Go web development.

title: Internship with AI start up: Tactile paving defect detection
exerpt: Solo project using Albumentations, OpenCV and Tensorflow to collect data and train a model.
content:
Worked on a joint project with LTA analysing and identifying defects in tactile paving tiles in Singapore.

- Organised and edited code using Jupyter Notebooks.
- Used albumentations and imgaug python libraries to augment image data, experimented with computer vision and data labeling.
- Dealed with CSV files extensively, used python to parse and extract data from labels.
- Used Tensorflow to train models for defect detection, experimented with both DenseNet and RCNN.

Scripts I've written can be found on my Github at https://github.com/Iscaraca/tensorflow-python.

title: Attendance taking app for schools
exerpt: A simple web app to experiment with Flask WTForms and SQLAlchemy.
content:
This project is for my school's Computing CA, where every student must design a web app on the topic of "smart schools". I wanted to make an app that demonstrates Flask-WTF and Flask-SQLAlchemy using a SQLite database, storing both accounts and attendance timings in two distinct tables, to familiarise myself with OOP concepts.

Students must log in first with a username and full name, then proceed to Confirm Attendance to book in with their username, class and a validation code displayed on the teachers' end. After confirming their attendance, their details and time of confirmation will be shown on Attendance Logs.

Teachers must display a unique validation code on their screens inaccessible to students. Accessing this code would require navigating to /password.html. The code will refresh every day at 00 00.

Website can be found at https://smattendance.herokuapp.com.
